# Pragma Glacier API — LLM Reference

> Historical financial data API for AI/ML research.
> Base URL: https://data.pragma.build/v1

## Authentication
All API requests require an API key in the Authorization header:
Authorization: Bearer glc_<your_key>

Get an API key at https://data.pragma.build

## Endpoints

### GET /v1/datasets
List all available datasets.

Response: [{ name, description, columns: [{ name, data_type }] }]

### GET /v1/files
List Parquet files matching filters.

Query params:
- dataset (required): prices, orderbooks, trades, funding_rates, open_interest, volumes
- from (required): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- to (required): End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- source (optional): Exchange/source filter (e.g., HYPERLIQUID, EXTENDED, PRAGMA)
- pair (optional): Trading pair filter (e.g., BTC_USD, ETH_USD)

Response: { files: [{ path, dataset, source, pair, date, size }], total_size, file_count }

### GET /v1/filters
Get available sources and pairs for a dataset/date range.

Query params:
- dataset (required)
- from (required)
- to (required)

Response: { sources: [string], pairs: [string] }

### GET /v1/download/{path}
Download a Parquet file. Returns 302 redirect to a signed GCS URL (15 min expiry).

Path example: archives/2026/3/15/orderbooks/EXTENDED/XAU_USD.parquet

## Datasets

### prices
Description: Aggregated price data from multiple sources
Columns: source (string), chain (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), price (float64), volume (float64)

### orderbooks
Description: L2 order book snapshots
Columns: source (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), bids (list), asks (list), sequence (int64)

### trades
Description: Individual trade executions
Columns: source (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), price (float64), size (float64), side (string)

### funding_rates
Description: Perpetual futures funding rates
Columns: source (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), rate (float64), next_funding_time (int64)

### open_interest
Description: Open interest data
Columns: source (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), open_interest (float64)

### volumes
Description: Volume data
Columns: source (string), pair (string), timestamp_ms (int64), volume (float64)

## File Path Pattern
archives/{year}/{month}/{day}/{dataset}/{source}/{pair}.parquet
Example: archives/2026/3/15/orderbooks/HYPERLIQUID/BTC_USD.parquet

## Python SDK
pip install pragma-glacier

```python
from pragma_glacier import GlacierClient

client = GlacierClient()  # uses GLACIER_API_KEY env var
datasets = client.list_datasets()
files = client.list_files(dataset="orderbooks", start="2026-03-01", end="2026-03-15")
client.download(files, output_dir="./data", parallel=4)
df = client.to_polars(dataset="trades", start="2026-03-01", end="2026-03-15")
```

## CLI
```bash
pip install pragma-glacier
glacier auth login
glacier datasets
glacier files --dataset orderbooks --from 2026-03-01 --to 2026-03-15
glacier download --dataset orderbooks --from 2026-03-01 --to 2026-03-15 -o ./data
```

## Tiers
| | Free | Pro ($99/mo) | Enterprise |
|--|------|-------------|------------|
| Datasets | prices, volumes | All 6 | All 6 |
| History | 30 days | Full | Full |
| Sources | HYPERLIQUID | All | All |
| Rate limit | 60/min | 600/min | 6000/min |
| Download | 5 GB/day | 100 GB/day | Unlimited |

## Errors
All errors return JSON: { "error": "message", "code": "ERROR_CODE" }
- 401: Invalid or missing API key
- 403: Tier restriction (dataset, date range, or source not allowed)
- 429: Rate limit exceeded (check Retry-After header)
